title: Pan-Seared Sea Bass with Sweet Treat Sauce
description: A German-inspired dish featuring pan-seared sea bass with a sweet and tangy sauce. Perfect for a special occasion or romantic dinner.
instructions: Season the sea bass fillets with salt and pepper.
In a large skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the sea bass fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the sea bass is cooking, prepare the sweet treat sauce by whisking together the honey, lemon juice, and remaining 1 tablespoon of butter in a small bowl.
Remove the skillet from the oven and brush the sweet treat sauce over the top of each sea bass fillet.
Return the skillet to the stovetop and cook over medium heat for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce is caramelized and bubbly.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve immediately.
Serve with roasted vegetables or a side salad for a complete meal.
